Collaboration: In addition to these improvements in details, we’ve also introduced a new collaboration feature: you can directly start a Microsoft Teams chat or call with the person who created a task shown in the To-Dos section:

Figure 27: Chat, start a video call or a voice-only call with the creator of a task, via Microsoft Teams.

Sharing your layout: Our users have also asked whether they can share their My Home personalization with colleagues, in other words the favorites, the groups, the choice of Insight tiles and cards and the overall layout: with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, this is now possible, via exporting to and importing from a local file.

SAP Fiori Launchpad

Important! As an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer, you will need to move from the Classic Home page, with its many groups of tiles, to Spaces and Pages by June 2024! Have a look at this SAP Note for more information, or also look at these:

  • Deactivation of Groups in the SAP Fiori Launchpad in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition: Switching to Spaces and Pages Mandatory.
  • Getting Familiar with Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • Video: Switching from Groups to Spaces and Pages (4:45).

SAP Fiori Launchpad: Merging Spaces, Pages and Sections

Since the 2302 release, customers can use the SAP-defined spaces and pages out-of-the-box, giving users access to their main applications without any set-up effort. 

Now, customers can go a step further and choose to merge spaces, pages and even sections: you can merge those from SAP as well as your own custom ones. Why would you want to do that? Basically, if a user has a few spaces which don’t contain many pages, or pages which don’t have many sections, you can optimize their navigation by merging them. Also, in some cases users can end up with pages or sections with the same name, so it would make sense for users to have these merged into just one page or section.

The layout is automatically adjusted, so this can be done with low effort. Figure 28 shows an example, where two spaces (“Billing” and “Customer Returns”), each with only one page, have been merged into one space (“Billing”), and where the two pages have been merged into one page. The two sections “Customer Returns” and “Flexible Analysis” from the “Customer Returns” page are automatically added behind the section “Billing Documents and Billing Document Requests” from the ”Billing” page.

Figure 28: Merging spaces and pages.

Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Merging Spaces
  • Merging Pages

Calendar Week Handling in User Settings

Users can now choose how they want dates to be displayed in calendars, for example when choosing a date via value-help. In addition to using the default format from your system, you can now select between ISO_8601 format, Middle Eastern and Western Traditional, as you can see in Figure 29.

Figure 29: Calendar week handling via the SAP Fiori launchpad user settings.

User ID Displayed in User Settings

I’m glad to say that we now support one of our main customer requests: users can see their technical user ID in the SAP Fiori launchpad settings section, on the tab User Account. This helps in cases where support is needed, since the technical ID is logged rather than the user’s name.

Collaboration: New Capabilities, Built-in and with Microsoft Teams and Microsoft Loop

To make it easier for users to collaborate with colleagues, we are introducing further capabilities with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.

Collaborative Draft

Some selected applications support collaborative draft handling of business objects. This means that more than one user can edit a business object, such as a payment advice, at the same time, and see changes made by others before the object is finally saved.  

Within the object page, the object header shows the avatars of those who are editing; you also get the avatar shown next to fields which they are editing. Also, we have extended the grid table to indicate which entries are currently in a draft state, as well as showing those currently being edited by someone else (see Figure 30); this is also available in the responsive tables.

You can invite other users to join you in working on an object, as you can also see in Figure 30.

Figure 30: Collaborative draft. On the top left you see how you can invite colleagues to edit the draft with you. The image on the right shows how you can see who else is editing the object, in this case “Payment Advice”, and which field they are currently editing. On the bottom left you can see the new indicators in grid tables.

Collaborative draft utilizes the latest SAP Fiori elements for OData V4 technology.

SAP Collaboration Manager enhancements

SAP Collaboration Manager allows you to chat directly with colleagues, right from where you are in the application. Whenever someone chats with you, you’ll get a notification in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

We’ve added a feature which allows you to mark your favorite chats. You can also use this as a way to take notes on specific objects or applications if you don’t invite anyone else to the chat. You can easily see all your favorites thanks to the new “Favorites” tab, which also shows how many you have.  

Another useful feature is the “mark as unread”. Both of these features are shown in Figure 31, on the left.

You can now add insight cards to a chat directly, as shown in Figure 31 on the right. This is in addition to the existing capability of adding a screenshot from the clipboard or uploading an image file. 

Figure 31: SAP Collaboration Manager’s new features: users can mark their favorites and set chats to unread (shown on the left); they can also now add insight cards, shown on the right.

Collaborate Based On Contact Cards

Wherever a user name or e-mail address on the UI provides a contact card with details when a user hovers over it, users can now directly trigger collaboration via Microsoft Teams, with three options: chat, video call or voice call. Figure 27 above in the section on My Home shows an example. To see it in action, have a look at this:

  • Video: Collaborative ERP: Collabore based on Contact Cards (0:44). 

Share as Card with Microsoft Teams Collaborative Stage View

You can already share a link to an application in a Microsoft Teams chat, but now you can go a step further and share the application as a card in the chat. When the recipient opens the card by clicking on the “open” button, the shared application is opened within Microsoft Teams as a new chat window, which Microsoft calls the collaborative stage view. Users can work with the application and chat with colleagues in the same chat window. Figures 32, 33 and 34 show some of these steps, but to get the best impression, watch this:

  • Video: Collaborative ERP: Share as Card based on Collaborative Stage View (1:49).

Figure 32: Share as Card – showing how the user Jim selects this for his sales order.

Figure 33: Share as Card – this is what it looks like for the recipient, Alice, after Jim shared the card and later added in the chat “Hey Alice, can you please remove the delivery block? It’s urgent! Thanks.”

Figure 34: Share as Card – after Alice clicked on “Open” in the card, she sees the application in the Microsoft Teams collaborative stage view, next to the chat. In this example, she has already interacted with the app in-place and removed the delivery block, and is about to send off her reply in the chat to Jim.

Share as Card with Authenticated Cards (Microsoft Loop Component)

Available with the 2402 release to customers who participate in the Early Adopter Care program SAP S/4HANA Cloud - MS Teams Integration (you can register using via the link).

We are taking collaboration one step further by providing interactive cards which can be shared with colleagues via Microsoft Teams chat, as well as by including them in an e-mail for example. Technically, these cards use an adaptive card-based Microsoft Loop component. Because the cards show live data from the system directly within the card, and allow users to interact directly, the user is fully authenticated before displaying the card, to ensure that they are authorized to see and interact with the data.

Figure 35: Share as Card with authenticated cards (Microsoft Loop component): the card is embedded in the Microsoft Teams chat, showing live information from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and providing in this example two buttons for interacting with the system. 

Again, this kind of collaboration is best witnessed in a demo, here is a preview:

  • Video: Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Share as Adaptive Card-based Loop component (1:25)

Once we have incorporated feedback from the Early Adopter Care program we plan to make this generally available.

AI and Joule

AI-Assisted Object Creation Accelerates Data Entry

With the 2402 release, we provide AI-assisted auto-suggestions for completing incomplete sales orders after a successful pilot phase. We are now piloting it for creation of returns orders, via the Early Adopter Care program. This solution uses machine learning to analyze a customer’s historical data, for example from the last six months, and learns the typically occurring relationships between the values entered in the main input fields and the values entered into other related fields. As a result, when a user enters a value in one of the main input fields, the system can make proposals for values to use for the related fields, as you can see in Figure 36. The machine learning uses the Data Attribute Recommendation Service on SAP Business Technology Platform, which is hooked up to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 Our design approach to AI is to always let the user be in control. In this case, we highlight those fields which have proposed values in blue. Users can click on the field to see further recommendations, as well as the history of values they have entered in the past. They are in charge and can confirm the main recommendation or select or enter the value they want. The user can also proceed to create the business object and accept all of them with one click in a pop-up.

Figure 36: AI-assisted object creation for a sales order.

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition                                                      

As explained above in the section Entry Points, Joule and Apple Vision Pro, Joule is your copilot for working with SAP, leveraging generative AI (GenAI).

 We are starting a phased roll-out with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 for selected customers via this Early Adopter Care campaign. Do sign up if you are interested, we plan to contact registered customers and partners in early March. 

The initial scope covers:

  • Navigational pattern for apps in Finance, Procurement, Professional Services and Sales.
  • Transactional pattern, role-based for three personas: operational purchaser, cost center owner and shipping specialist.
  • Informational pattern providing quick and seamless access to user enablement content summarized in a crisp answer.

This presentation explains these three types of patterns as well as providing an overview of our AI approach in general, and a high-level description of the Joule technology stack:

  • Joule, the AI copilot in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Video: examples of the three navigational patterns for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects enabled for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Customers and partners can now use SAP Business Application Studio to develop variants of standard SAP Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as a modification-free adaptation of existing apps. The resulting variant is treated as a new application, with a new application ID and hence a new tile, but it reuses the standard app and adds custom changes. This means that developers can focus on developing the value add, without having to copy or modify the code from the standard app. This also has the advantage that the app variant benefits from future enhancements to the standard application. Watch this video to get an overview, and read more in the documentation:

  • Video: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects (0:44).
  • Extending an SAP Fiori Application for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Improvements in Details

Copy and Paste in SAPUI5 Tables

Users can easily select areas in SAPUI5-based tables now and copy the contents from the clipboard and then paste into other applications, such as an e-mail or a spreadsheet. The copy action can be triggered by a new button added to the table, as demonstrated in Figure 37, or via the keyboard.

Figure 37: Copy and Paste from SAPUI5 tables.

Sorting in Tables by Name, Not Just by ID

In tables, we often show the ID and the name of an entity in one column, as “ID (Name)”, rather than in two separate columns. With this release, the table sorting feature offers you the option to sort by name and not just by ID. Technically, this is a feature of the Smart Table in SAPUI5.

Figure 38: Table columns can now be sorted by name in addition to ID. In this example, the Company Code column contains values with ID “0001” and name “SAP DEMO Company”; you can now sort by either of these.

Enhanced Type Ahead for (Multi) Combo Boxes

When entering data in the system, users want to do this as quickly as possible. Being able to very quickly select an entry from a drop-down combo box or multi-combo box helps this a lot, and we have now made this easier by selecting all entries which have a match anywhere with what you have typed – and not just matching the first letters. Figure 39 shows two examples for the field Credit Check Rule: the entry is selected which matches the input somewhere – and the part which matches is highlighted in bold text.

Figure 39: Enhanced type ahead functionality for combo boxes and multi-combo boxes.

Save as Tile Supports Dynamic Date Ranges

You can now create dynamic tiles for your selected SAP Fiori launchpad pages which include dynamic date ranges in the selection. Figure 40 shows an example where the date range “This Year (01.01.2023 – 31.12.2023)” has been used to filter the list. From there, the user has called up the “Save as Tile” feature, resulting in a tile which always shows the values for “This Year”.

Figure 40: Save as Tile supports dynamic date ranges.

Select All in Tables

Users can now select all the visible entries in a list report, using the select all checkbox in the table header. Since users might think that all entries in the database have been selected, when in fact only the entries which have been loaded to the UI are selected, a warning pop-up is shown – as you can see in Figure 41.

Figure 41: Select all enabled by default for list reports.

Further Improvements

I would also like to mention these improvements:

  • Tree tables in list reports and object pages which are built using SAPUI5 with OData V4 now support edit operations.
  • Users can now create My Home Insight Cards from applications built with SAP Fiori elements for OData V4, providing a consolidated view of the application’s content.
  • Creation rows in tables indicate to users immediately if mandatory values are missing.
  • Creation rows in tables allow developers to disable fields which should not be open for entering data due to missing entries in other fields.

 

UI Design and Technology News – Web and Mobile

Design Improvements to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori

The improvements to the Horizon visual theme which I announced back in September are now available, in UI technologies as well as applications such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402. In the interest of space, I haven’t included explanatory images here; you can get an overview in my blog post:

  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori

Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori Updated: GenAI and SAP Web Components

We have revamped the design guidelines for SAP Fiori for web, with a first set of guidelines in the new subsection for designing for Generative AI in the Designing Intelligent Systems section. It covers:

  • Design Principles for Generative AI.
  • Building Trust in Generative AI.

We are releasing this early version of our generative AI design guidelines to share our discoveries early on with our broader SAP and UX community. The design guidelines in this section will evolve iteratively based on research, input from the community, and feedback from stakeholders.

Coming soon:

  • Designing Safety into Generative AI
  • Designing Sustainable Generative AI Experiences
  • Designing Effective AI Prompts

In 2H2023 we also provided a new beta section UI Elements – SAP Web Components, where you can interact directly with each web component, since they have been embedded into the respective guideline page.

Design Research: Gathering User Feedback

We continue to invest in gathering more feedback from our users. Most of our major products now have built-in surveys to gather direct feedback from a user on how they rate the usability and satisfaction with the product.

Beyond that, in order to get more qualitative feedback, we introduced the SAP User Research Panel in October, where any user can sign up to help optimize SAP’s product user experience. Find out more and sign-up here:

  • Your voice matters! Join the SAP User Research Panel.

SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori Elements

Most of the UI innovations mentioned above in the section on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are provided by the underlying UI framework SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements.

Some of these are exclusively available with the OData V4 version, namely:

  • Collaborative draft (mentioned above).
  • Editable tree table, where data can be edited directly, and also the structure of the table can be edited via drag and drop.

For the sake of completeness: these features are now supported for OData V4, which were already available for OData V2:

  • List Report / Object Page: Support for the history of recently entered values
  • List Report: Support for Copy & Paste in the Filter Bar
  • Value Help: provide type ahead support in Define Conditions
  • Object Page: Runtime Adaptation: Allow removal of sections and subsections

This blog post summarizes the major new features of SAP Fiori elements:

  • SAP Fiori Elements in 2023: AI, collaboration, and consistency combine for a better user experience.

Mobile Design and SDKs: Watches, Cards and Many New Components

Following our release of the respective design guidelines mid-2023 for smart watches, the technology became available in November with the introduction of the SDKs for SAP Fiori for Android 7.0 and SAP Fiori for iOS 9.2, along with the first release of SAP Fiori for Wear OS and SAP Fiori for watchOS.

Figure 42: SAP Fiori for Wear OS

Figure 43: SAP Fiori for watchOS.

End of 2023, we introduced the 7.1 and 10.0 releases of the SDKs for Android and iOS respectively, which featured numerous new components.

Also, we have enhanced the mobile design system by introducing the mobile card system for SAP Fiori for Android and for SAP Fiori for iOS. This means that designers and developers building cards now have guidance on how to do that for SAP Fiori on mobile devices.

The new card system for native applications revolutionizes the way cards are used, providing a convenient way to display content from different sources. It consists of a toolkit of nested components that allow you to create a variety of cards that meet SAP product requirements.  Whether you need list items, KPIs, or a data table, each card’s flexible header, body, and footer seamlessly transform a single container into various card types.

Figure 44: Variety of cards in the mobile card system, all based on one card container.

Find out more in these blog posts:

  • New Features in SAP Fiori for Android 7.0 and iOS 9.2 and the First Release of SAP Fiori for Wear OS and SAP Fiori for watchOS.
  • SAP Fiori for Android 7.1 and iOS 10.0 – Introducing New Components and Mobile Card System.

Final Words

As you can see, we have great momentum in bringing forward SAP’s user experience. I hope you as a reader had enough momentum to get to the end of this very long post! I’d love to hear from you about whether you like this format, of putting everything into one long post, or whether you’d prefer this split up into a series of smaller posts.

 Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community. 

For general information on design and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence and Technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies. With the start of this year, I have started to cover latest updates from not only AI but also the technologies that lay the foundation for AI and enable SAP systems to be smart. This update will cover the latest from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.

Let's get started with my YouTube video below on the quick highlights.

Video: Discover what's new in Artificial Intelligence and Technologies

Here are the highlights:

  • Introduction to SAP Business AI Approach
  • Generative AI at SAP
  • Introducing Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates from Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)
  • Custom Configuration of Review Booklets with Review Booklet Designer

SAP Business AI Approach

SAP Business AI refers to a suite of artificial intelligence (AI) solutions and capabilities developed by SAP, a leading provider of enterprise software solutions. SAP Business AI leverages AI technologies to enhance various aspects of business operations, including decision-making, process automation, customer engagement, and data analysis. SAP's AI foundation is laid on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Figure: SAP Business AI Approach

Key components and functionalities of SAP Business AI include:

  1. Intelligent Automation: SAP Business AI offers tools for automating repetitive tasks and business processes through AI-powered robotics process automation (RPA) and machine learning algorithms. This streamlines operations, improves efficiency, and reduces manual effort.

  2. Predictive Analytics: SAP Business AI provides predictive analytics capabilities that enable organizations to forecast future trends, identify patterns, and make data-driven decisions. This helps businesses anticipate market changes, optimize resource allocation, and mitigate risks.

  3. Conversational AI (Digital Assistant): SAP Business AI includes conversational AI solutions, such as Joule, that facilitate natural language interactions between users and enterprise systems. These conversational interfaces enhance user experience, and enable self-service functionalities.

  4. Intelligent Insights: Through advanced analytics and AI algorithms, SAP Business AI generates actionable insights from large volumes of data, enabling businesses to uncover hidden trends, opportunities, and correlations. This empowers organizations to make informed decisions and drive business growth.

  5. Personalized Customer Engagement: SAP Business AI enables personalized customer engagement by analyzing customer data and behavior to deliver targeted recommendations, offers, and experiences. This enhances customer satisfaction, loyalty, and retention.

  6. AI-powered Applications: SAP integrates AI capabilities into its enterprise applications, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, to enhance their functionality and performance. This enables users to leverage AI-driven features seamlessly within their existing workflows.

Overall, SAP Business AI represents a comprehensive suite of AI-driven solutions designed to help organizations optimize their business processes, drive innovation, and achieve their strategic objectives in an increasingly digital and data-driven world.

For more information, please visit SAP Business AI

Generative AI at SAP

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses are constantly seeking innovative ways to leverage technology for competitive advantage. At SAP, we understand the importance of staying ahead of the curve, which is why I'm excited to introduce you to our Generative AI approach.

Foundation Models

SAP's Foundational Models are not confined to text alone; they encompass speech, images, and structured data, offering a wide array of capabilities. From summarization and writing assistance to question answering and code generation, these models are designed to revolutionize the way businesses operate.

Tailored Solutions for Your Business Needs

What sets SAP's Foundational Models apart is our commitment to customization and personalization. We understand that every business is unique, which is why we tailor industry knowledge and generic results to meet your specific needs. Through prompt engineering and embedding, we ensure that these models are seamlessly integrated into your existing workflows, providing precise and specific solutions for your business challenges.

Figure: SAP's Approach to Generative AI

Real-World Applications

The applications of SAP's Generative AI are limitless. From delivering intelligent and collaborative ERP solutions to interacting with Cloud ERP systems in natural language, these technologies empower businesses to streamline operations, access information, and solve complex tasks with ease. Whether it's improving the accuracy of document extraction, assisting in content creation and knowledge management, or boosting development productivity with code generation.

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is introduced with release 2402 in Early Adopter Care Program. More details below.

Introducing Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Starting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release, Joule is now available in the Early Adopter Care Program (EAC)*. This AI copilot is designed to revolutionize your interaction with SAP business systems. At its core, Joule aims to simplify every touchpoint and streamline tasks, ultimately enhancing your business operations.

Understanding Joule:

Joule is a comprehensive solution tailored to understand the intricacies of your business. It acts as your companion in navigating the complex SAP Ecosystem, offering transparent, role-based access to relevant processes from any location. With Joule, you experience a unified and delightful user interface across SAP's solution portfolio.

Key Features and Capabilities:

Joule facilitates conversational interactions, enabling seamless communication between humans and computers. It supports three primary conversational patterns:

  1. Navigational: Helping users find the functionality they need within SAP systems.
  2. Transactional: Assisting users in efficiently completing tasks.
  3. Informational: Retrieving relevant information from existing documents.

Moreover, Joule operates within various SAP BTP environments, including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, or others. It is a multitenant application, ensuring data isolation and independent provisioning for different consumers.

Have a look at the short demo video below.

Demo: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (EAC)

Enterprise-Grade Integration:

Joule seamlessly integrates with SAP applications, offering a rich web client compliant with SAP Fiori UI controls. Its out-of-the-box integration with SAP backend systems ensures enterprise readiness while maintaining compliance with AI ethics, GDPR, privacy controls, and SOC-II compliance standards.

Business Benefits:

The benefits of Joule are manifold:

  • Faster Work: Streamline tasks with an AI assistant that understands your unique role and serves as your copilot across SAP applications.
  • Smarter Insights: Gain quick answers and intelligent insights on-demand, facilitating faster decision-making.
  • Better Outcomes: Access excellent content for job descriptions, coding assistance, and more with just a simple query.
  • Full Control: Retain full control over decision-making and data privacy while leveraging generative AI in a secure environment.

*Availability with Early Adopter Care Program (EAC)

Joule is available in the Early Adopter Care program and more information can be found on the influence platform.

  • Registration Start:* January 9, 2024
  • Release to Customer:* March 11, 2024
  • Registration End:* June 28, 2024

Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)

Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) is a framework that allows you to perform lifecycle management operations on machine learning scenarios. You can consider ISLM as a self-service tool that allows you to handle various operation requirements of machine learning scenarios. You can train an intelligent scenario and use the trained model to get an accurate inference result.

More information on ISLM is available on SAP Help Portal.

I have two updates to share from ISLM.

Sales Order Auto Completion Use case

In the fast-paced world of business, efficiency and accuracy are paramount. That's why we are thrilled to introduce our latest ISLM use case: Sales Order Auto Completion. Leveraging cutting-edge machine learning technology within the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), this new scenario is set to transform the way sales orders are processed.

Introducing Sales Order Auto Completion

With Sales Order Auto Completion, users are empowered to streamline the sales order creation process like never before. By harnessing the power of Data Attribute Recommendation, this scenario provides intelligent recommendations derived from historical data, enabling users to complete sales orders with unparalleled ease and precision.

Key Features and Benefits

  • Efficiency: Say goodbye to manual data entry and time-consuming processes. Sales Order Auto Completion significantly reduces manual efforts, allowing users to complete sales orders in a fraction of the time.
  • Accuracy: With intelligent recommendations at their fingertips, users can rest assured that their sales orders are accurate and complete, minimizing errors and mitigating delays.
  • Integrated Data Management: Training the machine learning model is made easy with integrated data management handled via ISLM. This seamless integration ensures that users have access to the most relevant and up-to-date data for training purposes.

How It Works

Sales Order Auto Completion seamlessly integrates with the SAP Fiori app "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2", providing users with recommendations for input fields that are crucial for further processing. Additionally, the ISLM App Intelligent Scenarios Management enables users to train the model, upload data for training, view model metrics, and activate it for productive usage.

Watch the demo video below.

Demo: AI-Based Sales Order Autocompletion with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Availability

Ready to experience the future of sales order management? Sales Order Auto Completion is now available with SAP S/4HANA CFD 2308.3. Increase efficiency, enhance accuracy, and revolutionize your sales order processes with this game-changing solution.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Sales Order Auto Completion represents a positive shift in sales order management. By harnessing the power of machine learning and intelligent recommendations, businesses can streamline processes, reduce manual efforts, and drive greater efficiency and accuracy. Embrace the future of sales order management with Sales Order Auto Completion today.

Looking for more details? This innovation is covered in the blog post by Christian Vogler, Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 

Multi Model Management

In today's dynamic business landscape, adaptability and flexibility are key to staying ahead. That's why I am excited to announce this latest feature within Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM): Multi Model Management. This latest enhancement empowers users to activate multiple concurrent models based on specific business contexts, revolutionizing the way models are managed and deployed.

Introducing Multi Model Management

With Multi Model Management, ISLM users can now activate various model versions simultaneously, each tailored to different business contexts. This capability opens up a world of possibilities, allowing organizations to align their AI models with specific segments and fields, thereby maximizing their relevance and effectiveness. The screenshot below shows the Multi model check box.

Image: Multi Model Management in ISLM

Key Benefits and Value Proposition

  • Enhanced Flexibility: Gone are the days of one-size-fits-all model deployment. With Multi Model Management, organizations can activate different model versions based on values for primary segment fields and segment fields, ensuring that each model is finely tuned to its respective business context.
  • Visibility and Control: Gain insight into the activation context for each model, providing greater transparency and control over the model deployment process.

Custom Configuration of Review Booklets with Review Booklet Designer

* Note: the following is section is taken from the blog post Finance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 by Ulrich Hauke where it is covered in greater depth.

Using the Review Booklet Designer, analytics key users can now create custom review booklets. They use this new functionality in the Manage KPIs and Reports app to copy the SAP-delivered review booklet (Group Financial Statements Review Booklet) and adapt it to your reporting needs. In this process, you connect the content of an analytical query with measures, structures, and dimensions and display the results on a business page of the review booklet.

Value Proposition 

  • Create customer-specific reporting experiences and improve the adaptability of reporting content making use of a richer feature set.

Review Booklet Designer

Capabilities 

  • Use the analytical queries that are part of the standard review booklet
    • Define the content of business pages with rows, columns, and filters
    • Define variables and default values
    • Configure semantic styles and navigation targets

For more information, see: Review Booklet Designer, Creating a Review Booklet in the Review Booklet Designer

-----------------------------------

Further Information and Links to Useful Resources

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series!

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series
  • Explore the ready-to-run Cloud ERP .
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: 
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel
  • Become an early adopter for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Check out the SAP Activate Roadmap for early adopters 
  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...
  • Listen to our podcast: Inside S/4HANA Podcast Series
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Check out what’s new
  • Find SAP help here: 
  • Read the Feature Scope Description 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself via X @stalalhussain and LinkedIn - Syed Talal Hussain

entry.title: 7 Current and Emerging Trends in AI for Business
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become an integral part of the business landscape, revolutionizing industries and driving innovation. As we look at what’s already available and what’s to come, it’s evident that AI will continue to evolve and shape the way organizations operate.

Let’s take a look at seven current and emerging trends in AI for business, and the impact it will make for your organization:

1. Enhanced Natural Language Processing:

Natural Language Processing (NLP) has made significant strides in recent years and we can expect even more advanced capabilities. AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants will become more conversational, understanding context and intent with greater accuracy. This will enable businesses to provide personalized customer experiences and streamline customer support operations.

2. Reinforcement Learning and Autonomous Systems:

Reinforcement learning, a branch of AI that enables machines to learn through trial and error, is quickly gaining prominence. Autonomous systems, such as self-driving vehicles and robotic process automation, will become more sophisticated and reliable. This will lead to increased efficiency, reduced costs, and safer operations across industries like transportation, logistics, and manufacturing.

3. Explainable AI:

As AI becomes more pervasive, the need for transparency and accountability will grow. Explainable AI techniques will gain traction, allowing businesses to understand and interpret the decisions made by AI systems. This will be particularly crucial in sectors like finance, healthcare, and legal, where regulatory compliance and ethical considerations are paramount.

4. Edge Computing and AI:

Edge computing, which involves processing data closer to the source rather than relying on cloud infrastructure, will become more prevalent. This trend will be driven by the need for real-time decision-making and reduced latency. AI algorithms deployed at the edge will enable faster insights and actions, benefiting applications like autonomous vehicles, IoT devices, and remote monitoring systems.

5. AI-Powered Cybersecurity:

With the increasing sophistication of cyber threats, AI will play a crucial role in bolstering cybersecurity defenses. AI algorithms will be used to detect anomalies, identify patterns, and proactively respond to potential attacks. This will help organizations stay ahead of cybercriminals and protect sensitive data, ensuring business continuity and customer trust.

6. Personalized Marketing and Recommendation Systems:

AI-powered recommendation systems will continue to evolve, enabling businesses to deliver highly personalized marketing experiences. By analyzing vast amounts of customer data, AI algorithms will generate tailored recommendations, promotions, and advertisements. This will enhance customer engagement, drive conversions, and improve overall marketing effectiveness.

7. AI Ethics and Responsible AI:

As AI becomes more pervasive, ethical considerations will become increasingly important. Businesses will focus on developing and implementing frameworks for responsible AI usage. This includes addressing biases in AI algorithms, ensuring fairness and inclusivity, and establishing guidelines for AI governance. Organizations that prioritize ethical and responsible AI practices will not only mitigate risks but also build trust with customers and stakeholders.

From enhanced natural language processing and autonomous systems to explainable AI and personalized marketing, these AI trends will reshape industries and unlock new opportunities. However, it is crucial for businesses to adopt these technologies responsibly, considering ethical implications and ensuring transparency. By embracing these current and emerging trends, organizations can gain a competitive edge, drive innovation, and thrive in the AI-powered future.

SAP gives customers AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Learn about SAP Business AI
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI co-pilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: Revolutionizing Sales Efficiency with Joule: AI-Powered Intelligent Order Management in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
This Article has been moved. Please use the link below - 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/revolutionising-sap-s-4hana-sales-unleashing-intelligent-order-management/ba-p/13609594

entry.title: Revolutionising SAP S/4HANA Sales: Unleashing Intelligent Order Management Powered by Joule & AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, where customers demand seamless experiences and personalized interactions, companies are increasingly turning to advanced technologies to streamline their operations and enhance customer satisfaction. One such technology revolutionizing the sales domain is SAP S/4HANA Sales coupled with Artificial Intelligence (AI). This innovative combination is transforming traditional order management processes into intelligent, data-driven operations that deliver significant improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction.

First, Let’s See how the traditional Sales process works and its challenges…

The sales order process is the heartbeat of any organization. It directly impacts customer relationships, revenue generation, and overall business performance. However, traditional sales order processing techniques often present challenges, especially due to incomplete sales order data. Let’s get into some of the pain points faced by businesses:

  1. Manual Data Entry Errors: When sales representatives manually input data into systems, human error is inevitable. Missing or inaccurate information can lead to shipping delays, improper product deliveries, and negative customer experiences.
  2. Time-Consuming Data Validation: Verifying and cross-referencing data from different sources consumes valuable time. Sales teams often spend excessive hours validating customer details, product codes, and pricing information.
  3. Order Processing Delays: Incomplete data not only slows down order processing but can also result in order rejection or cancellation. Such delays create friction in customer relationships and impact the company’s credibility.
  4. Customer Frustration: Customers expect a seamless buying experience. Delays caused by incomplete data can frustrate customers, leading to dissatisfaction and potential loss of business.

Let’s explore & see, how SAP S/4HANA intelligent Order management is a game changer for this situation …

SAP S/4HANA Sales, provides businesses with a comprehensive platform for managing sales order fulfillment. When integrated with AI capabilities, such as machine learning and natural language processing, SAP S/4HANA Sales becomes even more powerful, enabling organizations to analyze vast amounts of data, automate repetitive tasks, and make data-driven decisions in real time.

Here, we need to understand the impact of Joule on business processes : 

What is Joule –

Joule is a transformative natural-language, generative AI copilot designed to revolutionize the way businesses operate within SAP’s cloud portfolio. 

  • It’s an AI companion
  • It’s embedded throughout SAP’s cloud applications.
  • It’s a trusted AI chatbot that enhances daily operations.

How it works :

  • Joule leverages Gen AI capabilities
  • It learns the pattern from existing data and proposes intelligent insight.

How customers use Joule :

  • Joule can categorize and contextualize information from multiple sources.
  • It assists users in various ways, such as executing finance operations or changing sales document entries.
  • It boosts productivity by granting quick and seamless access to user enablement content.

Let’s see a glimpse of the Intelligent Order Management process in SAP S/4HANA –

Fig 1 : Manage Sales Order : Version 2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The user creates/searches for sales orders in the new "Manage Sales Order: Version 2" app.  This app is enhanced with Microsoft Teams integration. This functionality allows users to share business info with related colleagues with screen data. 

Fig 2: Microsoft Teams IntegrationFig 3: Sharing Business InfoFig 4: Shared info with Screen data

 

The User now can activate/check "Joule". Joule can be immensely helpful to users with all business-related queries. With some continuous prompts, users can get their required business info/guidance to proceed further. In this case, the user has asked for information about the possibility of extracting information from PDF. With subsequent prompts, Joule has guided the user directly to navigate the Sales order auto extraction app to create a sales order by uploading a PDF. Refer to my blog How to create sales orders from PDF data? 

Fig 5: Activate JouleFig 6: Joule's Response to PromptFig 7: Subsequent prompt to JouleFig 8: Response based on PromptFig 9: Create Sales order : AutoExtraction appFig 10: Upload PDF data to create OrderFig 11: Validate and create sales order

 

Now in case of wrong data in PDF upload or due to human error, the newly created sales order goes to "incompletion" mode. Here also embedded AI helps users to autocomplete sales orders and proceed further. For more details, please refer to my blog Sales Order AutoComplete with AI  

Fig 12 : Sales Order AutoComplete Notification and status

 

Customer Benefits and Process Improvement with Joule and AI-driven Approach:

  • Enhanced Efficiency and Accuracy

By leveraging AI-driven automation and predictive analytics, businesses can streamline sales operations, reduce manual effort, and minimize errors. Automated order processing and intelligent inventory management ensure faster order fulfillment, reduced lead times, and improved order accuracy.

  • Real-time Decision Making

SAP S/4HANA Sales integrated with AI provides real-time visibility into sales performance, inventory levels, and customer interactions. This empowers businesses to make data-driven decisions on pricing, inventory management, and resource allocation, enabling them to respond swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer demands.

  • Return on Investment (ROI) and Customer Satisfaction

The implementation of SAP S/4HANA Sales with AI capabilities results in significant cost savings through improved operational efficiency, reduced inventory carrying costs, and optimized resource utilization. Moreover, AI-driven pricing optimization and personalized recommendations drive revenue growth by increasing sales volumes, improving pricing margins, and enhancing customer retention.

  • Competitive Advantage and Market Differentiation

Businesses that embrace SAP S/4HANA Sales coupled with AI gain a competitive advantage by offering superior customer experiences, faster order fulfillment, and more accurate pricing. This positions them as industry leaders and enhances their brand reputation, attracting new customers and retaining existing ones.

The integration of SAP S/4HANA Sales with Artificial Intelligence is revolutionizing the sales domain by enabling businesses to transform traditional order management processes into intelligent, data-driven operations. As organizations embrace digital transformation, AI-driven order management becomes indispensable. Joule exemplifies how AI enhances efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction. By leveraging intelligent technologies, businesses unlock new levels of success in their sales processes. In summary, the marriage of AI and order management in SAP S/4HANA isn’t just a technological leap; it’s a strategic move toward sustainable growth and customer delight.

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Our fully-fledged cloud ERP is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and offers innovation and enhancements beyond measure. But even the most scalable cloud ERP would only be half as efficient without a seamless user experience. Therefore, we keep enhancing our product based on your feedback to offer you a next-level user experience so that you can thrive in today’s work environment. It is my pleasure to show you just how we do that in this release. Spoiler alert: Generative AI is involved. 😉

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402:

Horizon Theme

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208, we introduced the Horizon visual theme to offer you a fresh and modern SAP Fiori experience and to help you get your job done faster, more intuitively, and with better outcomes. Taking your feedback to heart, we’ve already made continuous improvements to the design in past releases. With this new release, we now implemented even more changes such as:

  • White input fields instead of gray,
  • Clearly identifiable buttons,
  • Improved contrast,
  • Reduced header size,
  • And more.

Read more about this in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Figure 1: The darker background on the My Home is only one of many design improvements to the Horizon visual theme.

Spaces and Pages

As announced in my previous blog post, the groups setup on the SAP Fiori launchpad is now officially outdated; spaces and pages are the only available layout option as of this release (read more in this note). Switching to spaces and pages has now become crucial to avoid a blank screen on the SAP Fiori launchpad–and note, the "switch" does not happen automatically, but has to be done manually. Read this blog post by Sylvie Charpentier on how to efficiently switch to spaces and pages. We are granting a grace period until June 2024 to all customers who haven’t switched to spaces and pages yet. During this grace period, there’s no guarantee that the group-based content is up to date. At the end of this grace period, SAP will change the settings in development and test systems first, and eventually also in production systems to display spaces and pages as default without the option for administrators and business users to switch back to groups. The administrators of affected customers will be informed about these changes in due time.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, we ship a new functionality to merge spaces and pages which can be used to e.g., reduce the number of duplicate apps, pages, or spaces for users. This can also be helpful if you want to enrich SAP-delivered spaces or pages with customer-defined content.

Figure 2: “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager” before merging.

Figure 3: View after merging “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager”.

Find out more in Kattia Jordan-Philipp's blog post.

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Switching to spaces and pages will not only give you a new layout option to structure the access to apps following work processes in an intuitive and efficient way; it also enables you to use the My Home entry page in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Users benefit from a personalized interface tailored to their needs, with important to-dos and insights readily accessible. Designed for efficiency across devices, the My Home ensures quick task execution through intuitive app organization. With the 2402 release, we offer an increased level of personalization to make the user experience even more straightforward. This includes:

  • The harmonization of blank state,
  • Consistent group creation via drag and drop,
  • A total re-design of the app section,
  • And an increased language support for system news.

For new customers, spaces and pages and My Home are the default and you will benefit from them immediately.

Figure 4: Discover the latest enhancements to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402. Click on the image to watch the video.

Collaborative ERP

The My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new collaboration feature with Microsoft Teams. Based on contact information in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can now directly trigger a Microsoft Teams chat or voice/video call and start collaborating.

Figure 5: The integration between My Home and Microsoft Teams allows you to easily collaborate across apps. Click on the image to watch the video.

And there’s more: Another feature of the collaborative ERP is now generally available! We enable you to share your most relevant business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats. Through the cards, recipients of the message can directly edit the data in the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Figure 6: Share business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats and start collaborating. Click the image to watch the video.

SAP Collaboration Manager

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2302, we introduced the SAP Collaboration Manager. This cloud-based, innovative collaboration feature replaced the human-to-human chat function of the former SAP CoPilot in the three-system landscape and offers a seamlessly integrated collaboration experience directly inside the SAP Fiori launchpad, increasing overall user efficiency. Since then, we’ve been continuously enhancing the SAP Collaboration Manager to offer more capabilities such as creating chats and inviting your co-workers to collaborate without any system switch over, taking screenshots and annotating or highlighting them, and linking your chat to any screen for reference. In this release, we offer even more enhancements:

  • Add My Home “Insights” cards,
  • Mark notes and chats as “Read” or “Unread”,
  • Add notes and chats to “Favorites”.

Figure 7: The “Add Insights Cards” function allows you to provide convenient access to analytical information, lists, graphs, and associated apps.

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, developers can work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) to create variants of standard applications. In an adaptation project, you can extend the original code to define changes that fulfill the specific business requirements of different business roles, user groups, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process. After deployment, both the original application and your adapted application exist in parallel and can be made available to the relevant users through uniquely name application tiles in SAP Fiori launchpad/My Home.

Figure 8: You can now create SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio to generate app variants. Click the image to watch the video.

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402

In my first paragraph of this blog post, I promised to talk about generative AI–so, let’s talk about SAP’s brand-new, natural language genAI copilot, Joule. Joule is a comprehensive solution designed to understand the complexities of your business processes and will completely revolutionize how you interact with the system. It provides transparent, role-based access to relevant processes, making interactions intuitive and seamless. By enabling natural conversations between users and systems, Joule enhances productivity and delivers valuable insights. The three supported conversational patterns are:

  • Navigational: Joule helps users find required functionalities within SAP systems,
  • Transactional: Joule assists users in completing tasks,
  • Informational: Joule provides insights based on SAP Help documentation data.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, Joule is taking off through an Early Adopter Care program and will be available for selected customers for initial testing. Read more about Joule and other AI innovations in this blog post by Syed Talal Hussain.

Figure 9: SAP’s generative AI copilot Joule revolutionizes how you interact with the system. Click the image to watch the video.

Customer Influence Page

Our top priority has always been ensuring a seamless and straightforward user experience. Reacting to your feedback and requirements is the key to achieving this. The main input channel where we receive your requirements is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross-Topics customer influence page. To make sure we collect all your requests, we emphasize the importance of the channel and increase the threshold to 10 to get a better focus. We review permanently and stop all improvement requests that are older than 18 months and have not (yet) met the current voting threshold. Based on UX importance, here is a status report of features that were delivered and that are still in process/planning stage:

  • S4HC Home page personalization: User CB ID to be shown under “User Account” menu (19 votes), 2nd most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release
  • Adapt UI – Enable ability to set field properties (i.e., mandatory, visible) (15 votes), 4th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Can be achieved through UI Adaptation Projects
  • Upload custom logo without having to use UI Theme Designer (2TV) (13 votes), 6th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Planned with next release (Q3/2024)
  • Enable a download/upload of the My Home app tile information (11 votes), 11th  most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release

Figure 10: Our customer influence page is the main channel to request requirements.

When I said we are offering next-level user experience with this new release, I wasn’t promising too much. Get more details about the innovations and enhancements mentioned in this blog post and much, much more in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Feel free to drop a kudo if you liked this blog post. Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. Looking forward to the next release!

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

🎯Where to Find More Information:

  • Read up on the highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/highlights-of-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-release/ba-p/13587480

  • Need more information on how to switch to spaces and pages? Find it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13578088 and here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-efficiently-switch-from-groups-to-spaces-and-pages/ba-p/13604299

entry.title: Exploring the Business Benefits of AI
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a transformative technology that is revolutionizing various industries. From automating routine tasks to enabling personalized customer experiences, AI is driving significant business benefits across diverse sectors.

There are numerous advantages that AI brings to businesses, highlighting its potential to enhance efficiency, productivity, decision-making, and customer satisfaction.

Enhanced efficiency and productivity

One of the key advantages of AI is its ability to automate repetitive and time-consuming tasks. By leveraging machine learning algorithms, businesses can streamline processes, reduce human error, and improve overall efficiency. AI-powered automation can handle tasks such as data entry, data analysis, customer support, and inventory management, freeing up human resources to focus on more strategic and creative endeavors. This increased efficiency leads to higher productivity, cost savings, and improved resource allocation.

Data-driven decision-making

AI has the potential to unlock valuable insights from vast amounts of data. By analyzing patterns, trends, and correlations, AI algorithms can generate actionable intelligence that aids in decision-making. Businesses can use AI to optimize pricing strategies, forecast demand, identify market trends, and personalize marketing campaigns. With AI-powered analytics, organizations can make data-driven decisions that are more accurate, timely, and aligned with their business goals.

Improved customer experience

AI technologies, such as natural language processing and machine learning, have revolutionized customer service. Chatbots and virtual assistants can provide instant and personalized responses to customer queries, improving response times and enhancing overall customer experience. AI-powered recommendation systems can analyze customer preferences and behaviors to offer personalized product recommendations, leading to increased customer satisfaction and loyalty. Moreover, AI can help businesses gain a deeper understanding of their customers through sentiment analysis, social media monitoring, and customer segmentation, enabling targeted marketing and better customer relationship management.

Enhanced cybersecurity

As businesses increasingly rely on digital infrastructure, cybersecurity has become a critical concern. AI can play a pivotal role in safeguarding sensitive data and detecting potential threats. Machine learning algorithms can analyze network traffic patterns, identify anomalies, and proactively respond to security breaches. AI-powered systems can also continuously learn and adapt to new threats, making them an invaluable asset in the fight against cybercrime.

Streamlined operations and supply chain management

AI can optimize various aspects of operations and supply chain management. Predictive analytics can forecast demand, allowing businesses to optimize inventory levels and reduce wastage. AI-powered algorithms can also optimize production schedules, logistics routes, and delivery processes, leading to cost savings and improved efficiency. By harnessing AI, businesses can streamline their operations, minimize downtime, and ensure timely delivery, thereby gaining a competitive edge.

The business benefits of AI are vast and diverse. From enhancing efficiency and productivity to enabling data-driven decision-making and improving customer experiences, AI is transforming the way businesses operate. By embracing AI technologies, organizations can gain a competitive advantage, drive innovation, and unlock new opportunities for growth. As AI continues to evolve, it is crucial for businesses to explore and harness its potential to stay ahead in today's dynamic and competitive marketplace.

SAP gives customers over 130 AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Read about SAP’s Business AI strategy
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI co-pilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: 5 Game-Changing Capabilities in Generative AI for Business
entry.content:
It’s no surprise that generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) is starting to have a profound impact on businesses. With its ability to unlock valuable insights, help make better decisions, automate tasks, and personalize experiences, the use cases are endless. As businesses increasingly recognize the potential of GenAI, key capabilities have emerged, reshaping lines of business and industries, and opening up new avenues for innovation and growth. 

Let’s take a look at five capabilities in GenAI that are changing the way businesses are run.  

1. Enhanced content creation: 

GenAI has transformed content creation by automating and streamlining the process. Businesses can now leverage AI algorithms to generate high-quality images, videos, and written content, saving time and resources while ensuring a consistent flow of high-quality content. 
 
2. Personalized customer experiences: 

Hyper-personalized customer experiences are now easily obtainable with GenAI. By analyzing vast amounts of data, AI algorithms can generate tailored recommendations, product suggestions, targeted campaigns, and more. This results in enhanced customer satisfaction and increased brand loyalty. 

3. Virtual assistants and copilots: 

The capabilities of digital assistants have become significantly advanced due to GenAI. These AI-powered tools can now engage in more natural and human-like conversations, understanding context and providing accurate responses – more akin to a copilot that understands every aspect of your business. Copilots can interact with customers, answer queries, perform tasks, provide personalized recommendations, handle customer support, and even streamline various business processes. 

 4. Simulation and optimization:

GenAI can simulate complex business processes and optimize them for maximum efficiency. By modeling different scenarios and variables, it can identify the most effective strategies and optimize processes accordingly, such as optimizing supply chain operations, logistics, resource allocation, or even financial forecasting. This can help businesses minimize risks, improve performance, and achieve better outcomes. 

5. Responsible and ethical AI: 

As GenAI becomes more pervasive, ethical considerations are becoming increasingly important. Businesses focus on developing and implementing frameworks for responsible AI usage. This includes addressing biases in AI algorithms, ensuring fairness and inclusivity, and establishing guidelines for AI governance. Organizations that prioritize ethical and responsible AI practices will not only mitigate risks but also build trust with customers and stakeholders.

Watch the video: AI Built into All Core Business Processes

As businesses continue to embrace GenAI, they can harness its potential to gain a competitive edge, drive innovation, and deliver exceptional value to customers. The future of AI technology holds immense promise, and its continued evolution will undoubtedly shape the business landscape well into the future. 

SAP gives customers AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction. 

Interesting in learning more?

  • Learn more about SAP Business AI
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI copilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: SAP BTP Ask Me Anything Series: SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Coming up next on the SAP BTP Ask Me Anything series: SAP Build Code. Get ready to have your questions answered live by our experts on March 27th – get all details about the session in this blog post. 

SAP Build Code was announced at SAP TechEd 2023 as THE game-changer for SAP developers. Why? Because it provides them with a seamlessly integrated environment for coding, testing, and application lifecycle management. And all that with generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, based on natural language descriptions, and optimized for Java and JavaScript.  

What's it all about?  

With SAP Build Code, you get guided experiences and prebuilt templates to quickly build applications and extend your SAP systems - watch the teaser demo. It combines the most essential services from SAP BTP and delivers a cohesive experience for developers and fusion development teams:

Fusion development is all about breaking silos between teams and empowering business and IT to collaborate successfully – and this is what combining SAP Build Code and the low-code tools in SAP Build, can help you achieve. You can learn more about how SAP Build and SAP Build Code work together in this blog post: Comprehensive Tools for Novice to Expert Developers. 

Why should I join? 

SAP Build Code is now on preview and will be generally available in March 2024!! 

This upcoming AMA is the best option for you to learn everything you need to know about the solution, including how to get access and what’s next. And, of course, you’ll have your questions answered by experts in the topic: Tim Back, Chief Development Architect for SAP BTP Application Development, Automation and Integration, Rupa Maity is a Senior Product Manager and Yuval Anafi the Lead Product Owner for SAP Build Code. 

Join on March 27 – 3 pm CET:

🔔 Add calendar reminder

💻 Link to YouTube live

If you already have related questions, drop them in the comments below. We’ll bring them to the session, so our experts can answer them LIVE. 

See you there! 

All the best, 
Maiara  

Learn more about SAP BTP and SAP Build Code:

SAP BTP | Community Topic Page
